Freezer warehouse
its the opposite here
its bad on summer
we are legally obliged to GET OUT of the freezer every 2 hours for 20 mins
In this case we get out every hour for 10 mins
yep
better stay in the airlock where its neither
tbh its way worse on other warehouses
on my very first one, our shutter doors were open 24/7 so it was constantly cold with like minimal wear since you sweat hard due to the demands of work
and in the summer (which I didnt get to experience) ofc its europe
There is no AC
no fans either, and since it was ambient warehouse, lots of food/alcoho/beverage gets broken or spoiled
and smells extra strong
and since it gets broken/spoiled, cleaners are on the move everytime
And what does that get us workers? Delays lol
Ofc not, this aint a white collar corporate job
Pro tip: if you want a short term job, and you can google map the location of a warehouse job
Check their parking lot
The smaller the better
my first warehouse job was bigger than costco by 3x
Shit look like a coal mining job
50+ ppl on a bus leaving to get inside this 1 rotation gate
And another 50+ getting out looking miserable
Barb wires and 30meter fence surrounded the place
such is the case to many warehouses
this is why smaller warehouses is better
You pretty much know everyone
And pretty much break some rules unlike bigger warehouses, more ppl= more eyes = more micro managing
